key_insight: IGA-ODIL replaces neural-network PINN parameterizations with smooth B-spline/isogeometric bases, yielding sparse Gauss--Newton optimization and faster, more stable residual minimization.
engineering_relevance: The approach offers a practical surrogate-solver framework for forward and inverse PDE problems where accuracy, conditioning, and runtime matter in computational engineering workflows.
method: Represent the unknown field with B-spline basis functions from Isogeometric Analysis (IGA).
Minimize a robust residual loss derived from ODIL and weighted Gram operators.
Exploit sparse Jacobians and Gauss--Newton iterations for efficient optimization.
evidence_signal: The method was benchmarked on Poisson, advection--diffusion, Helmholtz, Allen--Cahn, and inverse Helmholtz problems, showing orders-of-magnitude speedups over PINNs and CRVPINNs while preserving high accuracy.
watch_item: Performance may depend on careful spline discretization, problem conditioning, and implementation of the Gauss--Newton solver for stiff or highly oscillatory PDEs.
paper_title: IGA-ODIL: Optimizing DIscretre robust Loss with Isogeometric Analysis to solve forward and inverse problems faster using machine learning tools
